description: Consolidated collection of terminal progress indicators: spinners, progress bar character sets, and related animation data. Collected from open-source libraries, reference documents, gists, and blog posts across multiple programming language ecosystems.

entry_schema.name: Human-readable entry name
entry_schema.type: Either 'spinner' (animated frame sequence) or 'progress_bar' (character set for bar rendering)
entry_schema.frames: (spinners) Array of strings; each string is one animation frame
entry_schema.interval_ms: (spinners) Recommended frame interval in milliseconds; null if unknown
entry_schema.characters: (progress_bars) Object with fill, empty, head, start, end character fields
entry_schema.phases: (progress_bars) Array of sub-character resolution characters for smooth fill; null if none
entry_schema.indeterminate: (progress_bars) Character pattern for indeterminate/unknown state; null if none
entry_schema.completion_states: (optional) Object with state symbols: full, empty, succeed, fail, warn, info, completed
entry_schema.notes: Additional context about the entry; null if none
entry_schema.source: Attribution object with collection, url, license, copyright, retrieved date
entry_schema.also_found_in: (optional) Array of other source collections where this exact indicator was independently found. Each entry has collection, url, original_key, and license.
entry_schema.group: Category grouping. Spinners: braille, line, dot, block, geometric, arrow, toggle, bounce, scroll, emoji, novelty, text, symbol. Progress bars: ascii, block, geometric, decorative, phased, emoji.

entries.name: pacman syllabics
entries.notes: PAC-MAN progress bar from the ruby-progressbar wiki; Head character: ᗧ (Canadian Syllabics Carrier KHEE); Remainder: ･ (Halfwidth Katakana Middle Dot)

entries.name: incremental blocks
entries.notes: Uses sub-character phases for smooth progression; On Windows, falls back to 3 phases

entries.name: braille pixel
entries.notes: Braille-based sub-character progress; Inherits from IncrementalBar; Fills bottom-to-top, left-to-right across the 8-dot Braille cell

entries.name: shady gradient
entries.notes: Shade-based sub-character progress; Inherits from IncrementalBar

entries.name: horizontal fractional blocks
entries.notes: Most widely used for smooth progress bars. Supported by most modern terminals. On Windows, only DejaVu Sans Mono and SimSun properly display all fractional blocks.

entries.name: tqdm ascii phases
entries.notes: ASCII fallback. The space is the empty character, digits 1-9 represent partial fill (10%-90%), and # is the full fill.

entries.name: tqdm unicode phases
entries.notes: Unicode smooth blocks. Empty string for empty, then left-fractional blocks from 1/8 to full block.

entries.notes: the ideal 8-character increment bar graph

entries.name: braille double orbit
entries.notes: Two dots rotating in opposite directions around the 8-dot braille cell, always 180° apart

entries.name: braille cascade
entries.notes: Horizontal row of dots falling from top to bottom of the braille cell

entries.name: braille spiral fill
entries.notes: Dots light up one by one in a clockwise spiral until full, then extinguish in the same order

entries.name: circle fill
entries.notes: Circle progressively fills from empty to full, then empties again. Uses Unicode pie-slice characters.

entries.name: shape shift
entries.notes: Cycles through fundamental geometric shapes: circle, triangle, square, diamond, star and back

entries.name: concentric rings
entries.notes: Concentric circles pulsing outward and inward: dot, circle, bullseye, fisheye, filled

entries.name: triangle spin
entries.notes: Solid triangles pointing up, right, down, left in clockwise rotation

entries.name: arrow trail
entries.notes: A filled arrowhead bouncing back and forth among hollow ones

entries.name: zodiac cycle
entries.notes: All twelve zodiac signs from Aries through Pisces

entries.name: dice roll
entries.notes: All six die faces cycling as if a die were tumbling

entries.name: musical notes
entries.notes: Quarter note, eighth note, beamed eighth notes, beamed sixteenth notes

entries.name: alchemical elements
entries.notes: Alchemical symbols for quintessence, air, fire, earth, water, aqua fortis, aqua regia, and bismuth ore

entries.name: hourglass flip
entries.notes: White and black hourglasses alternating, as if the hourglass is being flipped

entries.name: ballot box
entries.notes: Empty ballot box, check mark, then X mark—cycling through validation states

entries.name: power toggle
entries.notes: Power symbol toggling between standby and on

entries.name: weather cycle
entries.notes: Full weather cycle from sunshine through storm and back

entries.name: growing plant
entries.notes: Seedling growing into a full tree: sprout, herb, potted plant, deciduous tree

entries.name: butterfly lifecycle
entries.notes: Metamorphosis: egg, caterpillar, cocoon, butterfly

entries.name: space journey
entries.notes: A journey through space: rocket, ringed planet, star, milky way, flying saucer, crescent moon, earth

entries.name: sine wave
entries.notes: A sine wave scrolling through vertical block elements like an audio visualizer

entries.name: light chase
entries.notes: A single filled circle bouncing back and forth among hollow circles, like a chaser light

entries.name: comet trail
entries.notes: A bright core with fading shade trail scrolling across, like a comet

entries.name: Newton's cradle
entries.notes: Newton's cradle: left ball swings in, transfers energy, right ball swings out and back

entries.name: clock pendulum
entries.notes: A pendulum bob swinging back and forth beneath a pivot point

entries.name: dot pulse
entries.notes: Single dot pulsing from small to large and back: middle dot, bullet, black circle

entries.name: dot orbit
entries.notes: A large dot cycling through three positions like an orbiting body

entries.name: rounded corner spin
entries.notes: Rounded box-drawing corners rotating clockwise: top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left

entries.name: double line spin
entries.notes: Double-line horizontal and vertical with box-drawing diagonals for a heavier spin

entries.name: T-junction spin
entries.notes: Box-drawing T-junctions rotating: top, right, bottom, left

entries.name: Elder Futhark runes
entries.notes: First eight runes of the Elder Futhark alphabet: fehu, uruz, thurisaz, ansuz, raido, kauna, gebo, wunjo

entries.name: Greek letters
entries.notes: First eight lowercase Greek letters: alpha through theta

entries.name: shade breathe
entries.notes: Block shade characters fading in and out like breathing: light, medium, dark, full, then back

entries.name: half slide
entries.notes: Half-block sliding clockwise: upper, right, lower, left

entries.name: tilde wave
entries.notes: Wavy fill for a fluid/liquid feel

entries.name: colon march
entries.notes: Colons marching over dots, subtle and compact

entries.name: pipe fill
entries.notes: Vertical pipes filling a bracketed bar

entries.name: nested circles
entries.notes: Fisheye (filled circle in ring) and hollow circles

entries.name: lozenges
entries.notes: Filled and hollow diamond/lozenge shapes

entries.name: music notes
entries.notes: Beamed eighth notes (filled) and single eighth notes (empty)

entries.name: flowers
entries.notes: Black florette (filled) and white florette (empty)

entries.name: sparkles
entries.notes: Black four-pointed star (filled) and white four-pointed star (empty)

entries.name: arrows
entries.notes: Small right-pointing triangles with a larger head, pointing the direction of progress

entries.name: circle phases
entries.notes: Sub-character resolution using circle quadrant fills: empty, quarter, half, three-quarter, full

entries.name: shade phases
entries.notes: Five-level shade progression: space, light shade, medium shade, dark shade, full block

entries.name: fire and ice
entries.notes: Fire emoji (complete) melting ice emoji (remaining)

entries.name: battery charge
entries.notes: Lightning bolt (charged) and white small square (empty) for a charging battery feel

entries.name: braille radar sweep
entries.notes: Two-cell sweep that gives a clockwise scan effect.

entries.name: line tripod
entries.notes: Compact single-cell spinner using heavy line caps.

entries.name: dot seesaw
entries.notes: One active dot rocking left and right across a 4-cell lane.

entries.name: block escalator
entries.notes: Dual-cell staircase pulse with mirrored descent.

entries.name: geometric pinwheel
entries.notes: Quarter triangles arranged as a rotating pinwheel.

entries.notes: Directional pair rotates through cardinal and diagonal vectors.

entries.notes: Simple two-state latch animation.

entries.notes: Single glyph pulse from light to solid and back.

entries.name: text checksum
entries.notes: Hex-like text spinner suitable for data verification tasks.

entries.name: symbol trigrams
entries.notes: Eight trigrams sequence used as a symbolic cycle.

entries.notes: High-contrast ASCII style for terminals without Unicode support.

entries.name: geometric crystals
entries.notes: Diamond-based bar with a distinct leading edge.

entries.name: decorative spark
entries.notes: Ornamental style intended for playful CLI output.

entries.name: phased moon

entries.name: emoji traffic
entries.notes: Simple traffic-light semantics for status-rich progress displays.

data.description: Various bracket/delimiter characters for wrapping progress bars.

source.collection: Unicode Standard - Block Elements, Geometric Shapes, and Braille Patterns

exceptions.reason: Unicode character reference with codepoints for quadrant block elements. Individual characters appear as spinner frames in other collections.

exceptions.reason: Library-specific template placeholder documentation for indicatif's format string system. Not a progress indicator character set.

data.wide_bar: Progress bar filling remaining terminal width

data.notes: The current position can be animated using the cycle function, e.g. (cycle . "↖" "↗" "↘" "↙")

exceptions.reason: Terminal escape sequence protocol for native progress bar rendering. Not a character-based progress indicator; uses OS-level escape codes processed by terminal emulators.

terminal_support.name: ConEmu
terminal_support.notes: Originator of the protocol. Shows in taskbar and tab title.

terminal_support.name: Ghostty

terminal_support.name: mintty
terminal_support.notes: Since v3.4.2 (Nov 2020). Also supports mnemonic params.

spinner_groups.braille: Braille pattern spinners using Unicode Braille characters (U+2800-U+28FF). The dominant family in modern CLI tools.
spinner_groups.line: Classic line rotation, pipe/box-drawing spinners, and half-line variants.
spinner_groups.dot: Dot-based animations: growing dots, scrolling dots, ellipsis, and point patterns.
spinner_groups.block: Unicode block element spinners: quadrant rotation, shade patterns, and fractional block pulses.
spinner_groups.geometric: Geometric shape rotation: circles, squares, triangles, arcs, and corners.
spinner_groups.arrow: Arrow-based rotation and animation using arrow Unicode characters.
spinner_groups.bounce: Pulsing, bouncing, and growing single-character animations.
spinner_groups.scroll: Multi-character scrolling, traveling, and wide animations.
spinner_groups.emoji: Emoji-based spinners: globes, moons, clocks, faces, and other emoji sequences.
spinner_groups.novelty: Complex themed animations: games, scenes, and multi-frame ASCII art.
spinner_groups.text: Text and alphanumeric character sequences: alphabet, numbers, scripts.
spinner_groups.symbol: Miscellaneous symbol spinners: card suits, I-Ching, CJK, mathematical, and other Unicode symbols.

progress_bar_groups.ascii: ASCII-only character sets that work on any terminal.
progress_bar_groups.block: Unicode block and shade element fills.
progress_bar_groups.geometric: Geometric shape fill/empty pairs: circles, squares, rectangles, parallelograms.
progress_bar_groups.decorative: Decorative and ornamental pairs: stars, hearts, diamonds, bullets, brackets.
progress_bar_groups.phased: Sub-character resolution phase sequences for smooth fill rendering.
